name: Newsletter signup block
description: The newsletter signup block contains the same content as the [Newsletter signup](/sites/construct/newsletter-signup) pattern but without the top rule and bottom padding. It is intended to be placed within a section rather than used as a standalone full-width section. The Newsletter signup block serves as a strategic engagement touchpoint, most commonly placed alongside related content such as articles, where it acts as a lightweight conversion step to keep users connected beyond their current visit.
usage:
Use the newsletter signup block when the signup needs to be embedded within an existing section or template rather than presented as a standalone section. It provides a low-friction way for users to stay connected without interrupting their reading flow, integrating seamlessly into the content and allowing users to opt in at a moment of relevance.

In most cases, pages that include a newsletter signup should use the full pattern ([Newsletter signup](/sites/construct/newsletter-signup)). The block is appropriate when the signup sits within a manually built section alongside related content – such as blog or resource content – or when it is part of a templatized page (e.g. blog or knowledge hub articles) where it is already included by default.

### Layout

The block supports two width variants. Both contain the same content – only the width changes. Use the appropriate variant based on the layout of the page or the section it is embedded in.

**2-column width**

![example-2.png](images/0d5fa10a-5e02-4c64-b1f4-a00ecb4df152.png)

**4-column width**

![example-1.png](images/27cca5fd-6c42-4033-a224-9c14ec88849a.png)

### When to use

*   Use the block when the signup is secondary to the content and should feel contextual rather than promotional.
*   Use the block when it needs to sit within a manually built section alongside related content — such as blog or resources content.
*   Use the block when it is placed as part of a templated page, such as a blog article or knowledge hub article, where it is already included in the template.

### When not to use

*   When no relevant content section exists, use the standalone newsletter signup pattern instead — don't group the block with unrelated content.
*   When the layout doesn't support embedded blocks, use the full pattern instead.
*   When the page is a regular marketing or long-form page with clearly separated sections and intentional conversion points, use the full pattern.
examples:
**2-column width**

Medium dimension

![2-m.png](images/7d32eed0-4d50-4594-9a95-47c078343dd7.png)

Small dimension: a muted rule appears above the block to provide visual separation

![2-s.png](images/39df180b-42ec-4188-b157-6fc5a59d8d24.png)

**4-column width**

Medium dimension: a muted rule appears above the block to provide visual separation.

![4-m.png](images/d08b8ba9-a6f4-458e-bcef-a5f3a3cbc639.png)

small dimension: a muted rule appears above the block to provide visual separation.

![4-s.png](images/f1504bf3-f1f3-48b4-ab39-76b6dfc6aee3.png)

table.name: Heading
table.description: The block heading communicates the primary subject or theme of the section.

table.name: Body
table.description: Body text providing context for the signup. One or more paragraphs.

table.name: Label + input
table.description: A single-line text [Input](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-9t3OC1fOBH) for the user's email address, paired with a visible label

table.name: Checkbox + label
table.description: A [Checkbox](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-hES7I02ImG) paired with a label confirming the user's consent to receive communications.

table.name: Addendum
table.description: Additional content to include in the form, such as a disclaimer or supplementary information. Paragraph-style content.

table.name: Button
table.description: A single [Button](wiki-ref://grid-ymtdOF6n4R/i-nsWL1xi6H5) that submits the form.

properties.name: Body
properties.description: Paragraph-style content. One or more paragraphs.

properties.name: Input label
properties.description: Label for the email input field.

properties.name: Addendum
properties.description: Paragraph-style content to include in the form, such as a disclaimer or supplementary information.

properties.name: Button
properties.description: The CTA using the default button style. It is used for most actions that are positive but not primary, and may serve as the main CTA in non-hero sections.

properties.name: State
properties.description: The state the input is currently in.

properties.name: Input validation type
properties.description: If the input is being validated what type of input validation it is.

properties.name: Input validation text
properties.description: If the input is being validated the outcome / reason of the input validation

properties.name: Size
properties.description: The vertical density of the input element

properties.name: Text
properties.description: The text that is in the input field.

properties.name: Placeholder
properties.description: The placeholder text that is being shown if there has not been any text that has been entered yet.

properties.name: Checked
properties.description: In which checked state the checkbox currently is in.

properties.name: State
properties.description: The state the checkbox is currently in. Note that in the checked and child checked state there is no hover effect.
